operations.value: In a four-week randomized placebo-controlled trial of adults reporting poor sleep, magnesium bisglycinate produced a small additional improvement in insomnia severity beyond a substantial placebo response; the result supports possible short-term benefit but does not establish treatment efficacy for chronic insomnia.
operations.notes: Direct formulation-specific evidence. Wording preserves the short duration, self-reported population, and placebo response.

sources.title: Magnesium Bisglycinate Supplementation in Healthy Adults Reporting Poor Sleep: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial

notes: Adds one cautious direct sleep claim for magnesium bisglycinate.
